After meals, immediately introduce a competing behavior before the urge has a chance to form â€” get up from the table, brush your teeth, chew sugar-free gum, or take a short walk. A craving is not a command â€” it is a wave. It builds, peaks, and fades, usually within 3 to 5 minutes. Urge surfing means observing the craving without acting on it â€” noticing where you feel it in your body, watching it get stronger, and then watching it pass. Each time you surf an urge without smoking, it gets slightly easier the next time.

Smoking is often tied to a habit loop â€” a cue triggers the urge, smoking is the routine, and relief is the reward. Understanding what your specific cues are is the first step. It could be a time of day, a place, an emotion, or even a person. Once we identify the cue, we can work on changing just the routine while keeping the relief.
